intro_md:
For a private member club company the size of Carmel Country Club, indirect spend spreads across many routine categories — the kind that, in most organizations, renew with little independent market validation.

This is rarely obvious overspending; left untested, indirect costs tend to drift above market — quietly eroding the operating surplus that funds reinvestment, capital improvements, and the member experience.

reasons.title: Where Indirect Cost Hides
reasons.body: Direct spend concentrates — ~80% of dollars in ~20% of vendors — and gets actively managed. Indirect spend runs the opposite way: ~20% of dollars spread across ~80% of vendors. Where spend fragments like that, pricing tends to drift above market and volume leverage goes uncaptured — which is what an independent test confirms or rules out.

reasons.title: Inherited, Auto-Renewing Terms
reasons.body: Agreements renew on incumbent terms — rebates, escalators, and fees set years ago. The value is recovered by re-testing the contract you hold, not by switching vendors.

reasons.title: A Member-Funded Cost Base
reasons.body: FY2024 revenue of $36.5M produced a $3.9M operating surplus. Every dollar of recurring indirect cost is dues pressure or reinvestment forgone — making sourcing a matter of stewardship.

reasons.title: Food & Beverage Is Not Disclosed
reasons.body: The Form 990 doesn't break out food & beverage — usually a club's single largest member-facing cost. Built from disclosed lines only, this understates the true base; F&B is sized directly in the baseline.

opportunity.basis_note: From Carmel's FY2024 Form 990 and ERA category benchmarks; validated in the 30-day baseline review.

observations: Operating supply (~$2.0M) is the largest disclosed line — concentrated enough that re-pricing moves real dollars.
Spend spans six-plus departments with no central procurement — where pricing quietly fragments.
Food & beverage isn't broken out, so the real addressable base is larger than disclosed.
The 990 shows what Carmel spends — not what it contracted, or when terms were last tested.

categories.name: Operating Supply
categories.why_md: The largest disclosed line. Course inputs, equipment, and operating materials are bought across many vendors — the category where consolidation and a market re-test return the most.

categories.name: Professional Services
categories.why_md: Among the least-tested lines in a club budget. Both scope and rate reward an independent market check, not another incumbent renewal.

categories.name: Office Supplies
categories.why_md: High transaction volume, little oversight. Consolidating fragmented purchasing is one of the fastest, lowest-disruption recoveries available.

categories.name: Maintenance
categories.why_md: Service agreements renew on incumbent terms across clubhouse and grounds. Re-testing scope and pricing at renewal is where the value sits.

categories.name: Utilities
categories.why_md: Tariffs, meter configuration, and billing errors are recurring sources of recovery — and independent of usage, so savings never touch operations.

categories.name: Insurance
categories.why_md: Coverage rewards periodic re-marketing — it tests the rate and the broker compensation built into it, which rarely surfaces on its own.

methodology.recovery_note: blend of ERA category rates; contract-timing haircuts applied at baseline

honesty.cannot_know_md: Built from Carmel's public FY2024 Form 990 and ERA category benchmarks. Contract terms and current pricing are not visible from outside — some lines may be recently bid, others may hold more room than shown. The 990 also omits food & beverage — typically a club's largest member-facing cost — so the true base is likely larger. A starting estimate, not a finding.
honesty.baseline_md: The no-cost 30-day baseline replaces these estimates with actual contract data — across every applicable category, not only the six shown — and quantifies food & beverage, which the filing omits. It confirms where the real room is and where Carmel is already competitive. The result is a finding, not an estimate.

reassurance: No savings, no fee. No upfront cost. Nothing changes without your approval.

source.filing: Carmel Country Club FY2024 IRS Form 990 — Part IX Statement of Functional Expenses
source.basis: Mapped functional-expense lines; direct compensation, depreciation, interest, and taxes excluded.

snapshot.headline: Your next capital project may already be hiding in your operating budget.
snapshot.intro: The opportunities most clubs miss are not the ones anyone is hiding. They are the recurring, unglamorous line items leadership assumes are already handled — the costs no single person owns, behind a market almost no one re-tests. At a club of this size, the drift in those categories compounds quietly, year over year, and rarely surfaces in anything the board reviews.

equivalent.unit_label: full golf memberships in equivalent annual dues

snapshot.horizon_label: toward capital projects over five years, with no new member assessment
